rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. OAKLAND RIVIERA GROUP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT RYAN MOONEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant's motion to set aside default is denied without prejudice. Defendant's notice of motion states that the motion to set aside default is based on CCP Sections 415.20, 415.90, 473(b), and 587. However, in his Reply brief, Defendant concedes that he erroneously cited CCP §473(b) as the basis for relief, and "instead is contending that this court does not have jurisdiction for default judgment to be entered as he was not properly served and did not have actual notice." (Defendant's Reply Brief in Support of Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment and to Vacate Order for Appearance and Examination, 6:18-19.) Defendant's Notice of Motion does not cite to any applicable statutes to grant relief on this basis. The court cannot grant different relief, or relief on different grounds, than stated in the notice of motion. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1124.) Defendant's request to vacate the Order for Appearance and Examination is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. POLICE CHIEF RE: OFFICERS RONALD MCGOLDRICK AND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Additional Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT'S Motion For Additional Discovery Plaintiff's motion for additional discovery under CCP §425.16(g) is denied as Plaintiff has not demonstrated good cause. Plaintiff has not shown that defendants possess evidence needed for Plaintiff to establish a prima facie case, or what additional facts Plaintiff intends to uncover. (See 1-800 Contacts, Inc. v. Steinberg, 107 Cal. App. 4th 568, 593 (2003).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. POLICE CHIEF RE: OFFICERS RONALD MCGOLDRICK AND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And Special Motion To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint Per Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM, OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And Special Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff's Complaint Per CCP 425.16 Defendants' special motion to strike is granted. Defendants have satisfied their burden of making a prima facie showing that the alleged wrongful conduct arises from protected activity under CCP § 425.16. Written and oral communications made by police officers within an official proceeding of the law falls within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute. (See Schaffer v. City and County of San Francisco 168 Cal. App. 4th 992, 1003-04 (2008); Bradbury v. Superior Court, 49 Cal. App. 4th 1008, 1116 (1996).) The alleged conduct of Defendants submitting a false Incident Report on February 21, 2013, falls with the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute because it is a written report within an official proceeding of the law. Plaintiff has also failed to establish that the uncontested evidence conclusively shows that Defendants' conduct was illegal as a matter of law. (See Cross v. Cooper (2011) 197 Cal. App. 4th 357, 384.) The burden then shifts to Plaintiff to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has not produced sufficient evidence to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on her claims. Defendants' request for prevailing party attorney fees is denied without prejudice. A proper noticed motion should be filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
